name: Thunderbird SECurity AntiVirus
author: Jan Bludau
description: Checking every Email Attachment at Hybrid-Analysis.com a service Provider that scanns with 70+ Antivirus Programs.

optional.name: file_uploads
optional.description: Uploads user-consented attachment hashes or files to third‑party malware analysis providers for scanning (Hybrid‑Analysis, VirusTotal).

data_practices.purpose: Malware analysis of attachments to protect the user's mailbox
data_practices.retention: Provider‑defined; the extension does not retain uploaded file contents unless explicitly requested by the user

optional.user_controls: Uploads only occur after explicit per‑message or per‑account opt‑in. Host permission is requested at runtime when an upload is initiated. Users can revoke opt‑in in the options UI.
examples: SHA-256 hash of attachment and filename

optional.name: url_and_ip_scans
optional.description: Scans URLs and domains (urlscan.io, urlhaus) and checks IP reputation (abuseipdb) from user‑visible message content or links for threat intelligence.

data_practices.purpose: Detect malicious URLs, phishing infrastructure and IP reputation checks to protect the user
data_practices.retention: Provider‑defined; only minimal metadata (scan id, verdict) stored locally by the extension

optional.user_controls: URL/IP scans are performed only when configured in settings (autoScanLinks) or when the user triggers a manual scan. Host permission is requested at runtime.
examples: IP address observed in headers or links
